discrete math

set

subset

set A is a subset of a set B if all elements of A are also elements of B

union

intersection

disjoint sets

two sets are said to be disjoint sets if they have no elements in common

multiset

a multiset is a modification of the concept of a set that, unlike a set, allows for multiple instances for each of its elements. the number of instances given for each element is called the multiplicity of that element in the multiset

ordered pair

given are mathematical objects, is an ordered pair, , as an example, the coordinates on a function is an ordered pair

cartesian product

cartesian product of 2 sets is the set of all the possible ordered pairs that can be obtained by taking an element from as the first in the pair and an element from as the second

domain

the set of all allowable inputs for a function, given a function , is the domain of .

codomain

the set of all allowable outputs of a function, given a function , is the codomain of .

image

the image of a function is the set of all actual output values. it is a subset of the codomain.
